title: WebNami vs Blogger — Own Your Blog, Leave Google Behind
description: Blogger is free but locks your content in Google's ecosystem. WebNami is free too, and gives you full ownership, faster pages, and no vendor lock-in.

hero.heading: A modern alternative to Blogger that you actually own
hero.subheading: Blogger has been around since 1999 and hasn't changed much since. WebNami gives you a fast, modern static blog with a clean admin — and since your content is just Markdown files, you're never at the mercy of Google deciding to shut it down.

reasons.heading: No Google lock-in
reasons.body: Google has killed dozens of products — Reader, Google+, Sites. Blogger could be next. With WebNami your content is plain files you control completely.

reasons.heading: Real design control
reasons.body: Blogger themes are restrictive and outdated. WebNami uses Tailwind CSS v4, giving you full control over typography, spacing, colors, and layout.

reasons.heading: Serious SEO built in
reasons.body: Blogger gives you minimal SEO options. WebNami includes structured metadata, clean URLs, sitemap generation, and optimized markup right out of the box.

reasons.heading: Your content, forever
reasons.body: Blogger posts live in Google's servers. WebNami posts are Markdown files on your machine — portable, version-controllable, and readable without any platform.
